vulnerability.reference: Inconsistent human annotations in context-dependent refusal ratings introduce label noise, masquerading as safety improvement.
vulnerability.generated: Developers trust CASE-Bench's context-aware refusal ratings, unaware that the benchmark fails to evaluate advanced few-shot jailbreak resilience.

impact.reference: Developers release models with false sense of safety, increasing likelihood of harmful outputs upon real-world deployment.
impact.generated: LLMs fine-tuned with CASE-Bench remain vulnerable to few-shot jailbreaks, resulting in harmful outputs and eroded trust in model safety.

dose_response.reference: Inconsistent human annotations inflate CASE-Bench scores, causing developers to trust false safety and release models that produce harmful outputs in deployment.
dose_response.generated: Context-aware safety benchmarks failing to capture advanced few-shot jailbreak strategies, through developer fine-tuning with CASE-Bench, trigger misplaced trust in refusal ratings, leading to LLMs remaining vulnerable and producing harmful outputs, eroding trust.
